AN EF-1 TORNADO TRAVELED OVER 5 MILES IMPACTING PORTIONS OF WEST-CENTRAL LINCOLN COUNTY TN BETWEEN THE TOWNS OF DELLROSE AND FAYETTEVILLE. THE TORNADO WAS ITS STRONGEST /100 MPH MAX WINDS AND MAX PATH WIDTH OF 250 YARDS/ ALONG JD SUGGS ROAD WHERE A 40X60 FOOT METAL BARN WAS DESTROYED...A TRAILER WAS ROLLED ON ITS SIDE AND NUMEROUS SOFTWOOD TREES /CEDARS/ WERE SNAPPED NEAR THE BASE OR UPROOTED. DAMAGE WAS ALSO FOUND DOWNSTREAM ALONG CURTIS ROAD AND HWY 274.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR LEIGHTON, SNAPPING TREES NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 6TH STREET AND HATTON SCHOOL ROAD. LARGE PINES TO THE EAST OF THIS LOCATION WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED ABOUT HALFWAY DOWN, ALONG WITH MINOR STRUCTURAL AND ROOF DAMAGE TO A RESIDENCE. A CAR PORT WAS LIFTED AND DESTROYED AND UNDERCARRIAGE FROM A SINGLE-WIDE MOBILE HOME WAS PULLED OUT AND THROWN INTO A NEARBY TREE. ADDITIONAL LARGE CEDAR TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE TORNADOS PATH.

*** 3 INJ *** THE TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF U.S. HWY 431 IN BOAZ JUST SOUTH OF BRUCE ROAD WITH SOME MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO BUSINESSES ALONG HWY 431. THE TORNADO TRACKED ESE CROSSING MCVILLE ROAD AND AL HWY 168 CAUSING MINOR TREE DAMAGE. THE TORNADO INTENSIFIED TO EF1 WITH WINDS UP TO 105 MPH AND WIDENED TO 300 YARDS AS IT CROSSED BETHSAIDA ROAD...UPROOTING AND SNAPPING NUMEROUS TREES AS IT APPROACHED ALEWINE ROAD...JUST NORTH OF ELLIS ROAD BEFORE CONTINUING INTO ETOWAH COUNTY.

*** 7 INJ *** AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON HAMBEY ROAD JUST EAST OF MCVILLE ROAD IN MARSHALL COUNTY AND TRACKED EAST INTO DEKALB COUNTY. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT TORNADO DAMAGE OCCURRED IN THE KILPATRICK COMMUNITY ALONG CR 479. VERY LARGE HARDWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED NEAR THE BASE ALONG WITH NUMEROUS POWER POLES. SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE AS WELL...WITH AT LEAST TWO COMPLETELY DESTROYED. SEVERAL CONVENTIONAL HOMES SUSTAINED MINOR TO MODERATE EXTERIOR DAMAGE IN THIS AREA.
TREES DOWN AND HOUSES DAMAGED IN A 5 BY 8 BLOCK IN BOAZ NEAR HWY 205
3 OR 4 TRAILERS WERE DAMAGED AT AL HWY 68 AND AL 168

AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG CR 539 NORTH OF CR 50 IN DEKALB COUNTY IN THE FYFFE AND SHILOH COMMUNITIES. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WITH THIS TORNADO OCCURRED ALONG TRAYLOR STREET JUST NORTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 50/GROVE OAK ROAD. TWO LARGE, WELL CONSTRUCTED FARM BUILDINGS WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED. ONE OF THESE CONTAINED METAL TRUSSES AND THE POSTS WERE RIPPED OUT OF THE GROUND. DEBRIS WAS STREWN SEVERAL HUNDRED YARDS AND LARGE DEBRIS WAS EMBEDDED IN THE GROUND.

A BRIEF EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE WEST SIDE OF MEMORIAL PARKWAY NEAR THE 9000 BLOCK OF CRAIGMONT DRIVE CAUSING SEVERAL LARGE TREES TO BE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. IT THEN CAUSED EXTENSIVE POWER LINE AND POWER POLE DAMAGE ALONG WHITESBURG DRIVE NEAR LILY FLAGG ROAD. THE TORNADO PROCEEDED NORTHEAST SNAPPING AND UPROOTING TREES IN SEVERAL RESIDENTIAL NEIGHBORHOODS BEFORE LIFTING NEAR HAVEN STREET JUST WEST OF BAILEY COVE ROAD.
DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO A MINI-STORE AT THE INTERSECTION OF LILY FLAG ROAD AND WHITESBURG DRIVE.

A BRIEF EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG DUG HILL ROAD NEAR CHEVAL BLVD WHERE LARGE CEDAR TREES WERE SNAPPED AND TWISTED ALONG WITH SEVERAL SNAPPED WOODEN POWER POLES. A ROOF WAS ALSO RIPPED OFF OF A BARN ON THE EAST SIDE OF DUG HILL ROAD WITH A LARGE SWATH OF CEDAR AND OTHER HARDWOOD TREES SNAPPED NEAR THE BASE...OR UPROOTED. THE TORNADO THEN LIFTED JUST NORTH OF RAINTREE ROAD.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF STATE HIGHWAY 50 NEAR CRYSTAL RIDGE ROAD CAUSING MINOR DAMAGE TO A BARN AND SNAPPING TREES ALONG MULBERRY CREEK. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST MOVING INTO MOORE COUNTY AND REACHING EF-1 INTENSITY WHILE SNAPPING AND UPROOTING BOTH SOFTWOOD AND HARDWOOD TREES. THE TORNADO REACHED ITS MAXIMUM INTENSITY OF 105 MPH AND ITS WIDEST PATH OF 400 YARDS. IT THEN UPROOTED MORE TREES...SNAPPING A POWER POLE...AND PEELED OFF A BARN ROOF BEFORE LIFTING NEAR BAKERTOWN ROAD.

TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN ALONG ROY LONG RD JUST WEST OF U.S. HWY 31. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED/UPROOTED AROUND RESIDENTIAL PROPERTY. MAX WINDS WERE DETERMINED TO BE 75 MPH...MID RANGE EF-0. THE TORNADO SKIPPED TO THE NE WITH ADDITIONAL DAMAGE SEEN ALONG U.S. HWY 72. MULTIPLE SIGNS ON A LARGE METAL POLE WERE BLOWN OUT...AND A PORTION OF METAL SIDING WAS RIPPED OUT. THE TORNADO LIFTED OVER HWY 72...ABOUT 1/4 MILE WEST OF HWY 31.

THIS VERY SHORT-LIVED TORNADO WAS PART OF THE SAME STORM THAT PRODUCED THE SOUTH ATHENS TORNADO. DAMAGE WAS SEEN IN A LESS THAN 1/4 MILE SWATH BETWEEN RESIDENCES ALONG EITHER SIDE OF HARDIMAN LANE. SEVERAL RESIDENCES SUSTAINED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE WITH SHINGLES BLOWN OFF. ONE RESIDENCE SUSTAINED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE AS A PORTION OF THE BRICKS WERE CRACKED AND BEGINNING TO PULL AWAY FROM THE FOUNDATION. SEVERAL TREES AROUND THE RESIDENCES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. MAX WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 70 MPH.

...POSSIBLE TORNADO...FRANKLIN COUNTY EMA REPORTED TREES DOWN OF UNKNOWN SIZE NEAR AND ALONG THE INTERSECTION OF OLD ESTILL SPRINGS ROAD AND LUTTRELL ROAD. ONE HOME SUFFERED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG LUTTRELL DR IN WINCHESTER JUST WEST OF ESTILL SPRINGS RD. LARGE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED OFF SEVERAL TREES. A HOME ON THE WEST SIDE OF HWY 41 SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE WITH APPROX. 1/4 OF ITS SHINGLES REMOVED. SEVERAL LARGE HARDWOOD TREES WERE UPROOTED AROUND THIS AREA. THE TORNADO REACHED ITS MAX INTENSITY HERE WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT AROUND 105 MPH. THE TORNADO HAD A MAX PATH WIDTH OF 150 YARDS AROUND THIS SAME LOCATION. PATH LENGTH WAS ESTIMATED AT 2.95 MILES.

THE SAME PARENT STORM THAT PRODUCED AN EF-1 TORNADO NEAR ESTILL SPRINGS ALSO PRODUCED ANOTHER VERY BRIEF TORNADO ALONG PRARIE PLAINS ROAD JUST NORTH GUM CREEK RD. SMALL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE ROAD. A BARN SUSTAINED ROOF AND MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE AS WELL. SEVERAL TREES WERE DAMAGED NEAR A HOMESTEAD ALONG OAK GROVE ROAD. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST NORTHEAST OF OAK GROVE RD IN A VERY RURAL PORTION OF FRANKLIN COUNTY. THIS WAS AN EF-0 TORNADO. MAX WINDS OF 85 MPH.
TREE LIMBS WERE KNOCKED ONTO POWER LINES KNOCKING OUT POWER IN HAMPTON COVE, OWENS CROSSROADS, AND THE GREEN MOUNTAIN AREAS IN SOUTHEAST MADISON COUNTY. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

15 AREA AND COUNTY ROADS WERE CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER IN EASTERN AND SOUTHEASTERN LIMESTONE COUNTY FROM NEAR MOORESVILLE ROAD TO ALONG AND NORTHEAST OF HUNTSVILLE AND BROWNS FERRY ROADS. WATER WAS AT LEAST AT FOOT TO A FOOT AND A HALF DEEP ACROSS THE FLOODED AREAS.

RISING WATER CAUSED A FUEL LEAK IN DOWNTOWN MOULTON. 200 GALLONS OF GASOLINE LEAKED OUT DUE TO FLOOD WATERS COVERING THE TANK. EMERGENCY PERSONNEL CONTAINED THE SPILL WITHIN ABOUT 15 MINUTES. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.
